name: TryLapse differentiation and wedge hypotheses
summary: Because the Claude share content was not readable, wedges are inferred for a TryLapse macOS timelapse recorder. Strongest hypotheses ranked: (1) Reliability wedge — export always plays in Quick Look, segmented capture, clear recording state in menu bar; (2) Trust wedge — app-only + sensitive-app blacklist + auto-pause on password managers; (3) Workflow wedge — presets for dev/design (15s interval, 30x speed, 1080p) and one-click share; (4) Brand wedge — TryLapse name aligns with try/demo narrative for indie builders; (5) Open-core wedge — MIT core with paid Pro for 4K/8K and smart idle.
key_findings: Incumbents leave UX bugs in reviews; TimeLapze covers technical OSS baseline; No dominant timelapse-native community platform shipped (Makerlapse teased); AI auto-edit is adjacent not core unless share proposed AI highlights
implications_for_trylapse: Pick ONE wedge for v1 — recommend Reliability + app-scoped capture; Name reinforces trial/build-in-public positioning; Avoid feature parity with Hustl 8K on day one
strengths: Clear gaps from review mining; Name differentiation vs TimeLapze/Lapse
weaknesses: Exact product intent from Claude share unknown — wedges may mismatch user vision
